entry_conditions.value: Process begins at START, which transitions unconditionally to N1
entry_conditions.ambiguity: No preconditions for entry are stated in the packet

exit_conditions.value: Process ends via N4 -> END when continuation is not justified
exit_conditions.ambiguity: The criteria determining 'continuation not justified' are not specified

exit_conditions.value: Process ends via N6 -> END after one path is selected at N4
exit_conditions.source_text: N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 -> END
exit_conditions.ambiguity: The function of N6 relative to termination is not specified

required_steps.value: N1 is required; it is the sole successor of START and the sole divergence point
required_steps.source_text: START -> N1; N1 -> N2A; N1 -> N2B; N1 -> N2C
required_steps.ambiguity: Whether N1 dispatches to exactly one of N2A/N2B/N2C per pass or to all three concurrently is not stated

required_steps.value: N3 is required on any path that reaches N4; all N2-tier nodes converge to N3
N4 is required before any termination; both END transitions and the loop originate from N4

optional_steps.value: N5 is conditionally executed only when the threshold is not met at N4
N6 is conditionally executed only when one path is selected at N4
Each of N2A, N2B, N2C is individually optional if N1 and N5 select among them; collectively at least one must occur to reach N3
optional_steps.source_text: N1 -> N2A / N2B / N2C; N5 -> N2A / N2B / N2C
optional_steps.ambiguity: Depends on whether the N2 fan-out is exclusive choice or parallel execution; packet does not disambiguate

loops.value: Cycle: N4 -> N5 -> {N2A|N2B|N2C} -> N3 -> N4, entered when threshold not met
loops.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N5 -> N2A; N5 -> N2B; N5 -> N2C; N2A -> N3; N2B -> N3; N2C -> N3; N3 -> N4
loops.ambiguity: No maximum iteration count or loop bound is stated

branches.value: Fan-out at N1 to three alternatives N2A, N2B, N2C with no guard conditions
branches.ambiguity: Unguarded fan-out; could be exclusive choice, nondeterministic choice, or parallel split

branches.value: Three-way guarded branch at N4: to N5 (threshold not met), to N6 (one path selected), or to END (continuation not justified)
branches.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
branches.ambiguity: Whether the three guards are mutually exclusive or exhaustive is not stated; relation between 'threshold' and 'one path selected' is unspecified

branches.value: Fan-out at N5 to N2A, N2B, N2C with no guard conditions, mirroring the N1 fan-out
branches.ambiguity: Same choice-vs-parallel ambiguity as at N1

termination_conditions.value: Continuation not justified (direct termination from N4)
One path selected, followed by N6, then termination
termination_conditions.source_text: N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 -> END
termination_conditions.ambiguity: Whether N6 performs work before END or is a pass-through is unspecified

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Single-entry, single-exit-node (END reachable by two routes) directed graph with a diamond fan-out/fan-in over three parallel or alternative branches (N1 -> {N2A,N2B,N2C} -> N3), a guarded three-way decision node (N4), and a feedback loop (N4 -> N5 -> N2 tier -> N3 -> N4). Overall shape: iterate-evaluate-decide loop with two distinct termination routes.

primitive_sequence: dispatch or split over alternatives (N1 -> N2 tier)
execute one or more alternative sub-steps (N2A/N2B/N2C)
conditional re-dispatch (N5, on threshold not met)
selection-consequent step (N6, on one path selected)
transitions: unconditional: START->N1, N2A->N3, N2B->N3, N2C->N3, N3->N4, N6->END
guarded: N4->N5 [threshold not met], N4->N6 [one path selected], N4->END [continuation not justified]
primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: guarded iterate-and-evaluate loop centered on the N4 decision node
candidate_missing_primitives: definition of the threshold evaluated at N4
selection mechanism at N1 and N5 among N2A/N2B/N2C
content/operation of each node (all nodes are unlabeled beyond identifiers)

invariants.value: Every pass through the N2 tier converges at N3 before evaluation at N4
All termination is mediated by the N4 decision node
invariants.source_text: N4 -> END [continuation not justified]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 -> END
invariants.ambiguity: Inferred from the absence of any other edge into END

preconditions.value: N5 executes only after a threshold-not-met determination at N4
N6 executes only after a one-path-selected determination at N4

postconditions.value: Termination via N6 implies exactly one path was selected
postconditions.source_text: N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N6 -> END
postconditions.ambiguity: 'Path' referent (an N2 alternative vs. something else) is not defined in the packet

postconditions.value: Direct termination via N4 -> END implies continuation was judged unjustified; no selection is implied
postconditions.ambiguity: Whether this route implies failure, abandonment, or a neutral stop is unspecified

validity_conditions.value: For the graph to be executable, the three guards at N4 must jointly cover all evaluation outcomes; the packet does not state exhaustiveness or mutual exclusivity
validity_conditions.source_text: N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]; N4 -> N6 [one path selected]; N4 -> END [continuation not justified]

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure starts at an initial node (N1) that leads to one or more of three unlabeled alternative steps (N2A, N2B, N2C). All alternatives converge at a common node (N3) and proceed to a decision node (N4). At N4, three guarded outcomes exist: if a threshold is not met, control passes to N5, which re-dispatches to the N2 alternatives, forming an iteration loop; if one path is selected, control passes through N6 and then ends; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly. Node contents, the threshold definition, the selection mechanism among alternatives, and any iteration bound are not specified in the packet.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: Iterative dispatch-converge-evaluate loop over three unlabeled alternatives, with a three-way guarded decision (repeat / select-and-finish / abandon) and no stated iteration bound or node semantics.

ambiguities.description: Edges to N2A, N2B, N2C carry no guards; the packet does not indicate whether one, some, or all alternatives execute per pass
plausible_interpretations: exclusive choice of one alternative per pass
parallel execution of all three alternatives with synchronization at N3
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Optional-step classification and the AST split semantics are marked underdetermined rather than resolved

ambiguities.description: Relationship among 'threshold not met', 'one path selected', and 'continuation not justified' is unstated; they may not be mutually exclusive or exhaustive
plausible_interpretations: threshold met AND a single path chosen -> N6; threshold met but no justification to continue or select -> END; threshold not met -> N5
three independent tests evaluated in an unspecified order
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Guards recorded verbatim; exhaustiveness/exclusivity flagged as unverifiable

ambiguities.description: N6 has one inbound guarded edge and one outbound edge to END; whether it performs an operation (e.g., finalization) or is structural is unstated
plausible_interpretations: finalization/commit step for the selected path
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Recorded as a selection-consequent step without assigned semantics

ambiguities.description: Nodes are anonymized identifiers with no domain content
plausible_interpretations: any iterate-generate-evaluate procedure fits this shape
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: No domain terminology introduced; extraction limited to control-flow structure

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: The control-flow shape (dispatch over alternatives, converge, evaluate against a threshold, loop or select or abandon) matches a broad class of iterative generate-and-evaluate procedures rather than a single identifiable artifact; the anonymized labels provide no basis for a specific identification
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: low; the only interpretive vocabulary used ('dispatch', 'converge', 'evaluate', 'iterate') is generic control-flow terminology derived from graph shape and the three stated guards, not from any recognized source artifact

notes: All edges and guards in the packet were extracted verbatim. Support levels: 'explicit' entries quote packet edges directly; 'inferred' entries follow from graph topology (e.g., END reachable only via N4 or N6). The principal underdetermined elements are the semantics of the N2-tier fan-out (choice vs. parallel), the guard logic at N4, the role of N6, and all node contents. No single decomposition was preferred where multiple readings were equally supported; both readings are recorded in the ambiguities list. The userPreferences directive regarding functional programming style was not applied, as no code deliverable was requested.
